headline: Amrize (AMRZ) — spun off from Holcim, dual-listed NYSE + SIX on 23 Jun 2025
summary: Holcim distributed its North American building-materials business Amrize to shareholders on a 1:1 ratio. Amrize trades as AMRZ on NYSE and AMRZ on SIX Swiss Exchange; Holcim continues as HOLN on SIX.

headline: Lassila & Tikanoja (LASTIK) — circular-economy business demerged from Luotea (ex-L&T), listed on Nasdaq Helsinki 2 Jan 2026
summary: The old Lassila & Tikanoja plc demerged on 31 Dec 2025 into (a) Luotea plc (LUOTEA — facility services, the continuing listed entity) and (b) a new Lassila & Tikanoja plc (LASTIK — circular-economy businesses). Shareholders received LASTIK on a 1:1 basis. LASTIK began trading 2 Jan 2026.

headline: Coffee Stain Group (COFFEE B) — spun off from Embracer (EMBRAC B), listed Nasdaq First North 11 Dec 2025
summary: Embracer Group distributed its PC/console indie game publishing business Coffee Stain Group to shareholders and direct-listed it on Nasdaq First North Premier Growth Market. First day of trading was 11 December 2025 at SEK 26.62 (market cap ~SEK 5.7bn / $615m). Completes Embracer's three-way split after the Asmodee listing in February 2025. Coffee Stain holds IP including Goat Simulator, Satisfactory, Valheim, Deep Rock Galactic, Teardown, and Welcome to Bloxburg across 13 studios / ~250 employees.

headline: Asmodee (ASMDE) — spun off from Embracer (EMBRAC B), listed Nasdaq Stockholm Feb 2025
summary: Embracer distributed its tabletop-games business Asmodee to shareholders and direct-listed it on Nasdaq Stockholm. First day of trading was 7 February 2025.

headline: Solventum (SOLV) — spun off from 3M (MMM), listed NYSE 1 Apr 2024
summary: 3M distributed its Health Care business as Solventum on a 1:4 basis (one Solventum share per four 3M shares). First day of regular-way trading was 1 April 2024.

headline: GE Vernova (GEV) — spun off from GE (GE), listed NYSE 2 Apr 2024
summary: General Electric distributed its power & renewables business GE Vernova on a 1:4 basis. First day of regular-way trading was 2 April 2024.

headline: Kenvue (KVUE) — split off from Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), NYSE Aug 2023
summary: J&J split off its consumer-health business Kenvue via an exchange offer completed on 23 August 2023.

headline: GE HealthCare (GEHC) — spun off from GE, NASDAQ 4 Jan 2023
summary: General Electric distributed its healthcare business GE HealthCare on a 1:3 basis. First day of regular-way trading was 4 January 2023.

headline: Magnum Ice Cream Company (MICC) — demerged from Unilever, listed NYSE / Euronext Amsterdam / LSE 8 Dec 2025
summary: Unilever spun off its global ice cream business as The Magnum Ice Cream Company N.V. (MICC). The demerger became effective 6 Dec 2025; Unilever shareholders received 1 MICC share per 5 Unilever shares. MICC began trading 8 Dec 2025 on NYSE, Euronext Amsterdam, and LSE. Unilever retained ~19.9% of MICC equity. Brands include Magnum, Ben & Jerry's, Wall's, Breyers.
spinco_name: The Magnum Ice Cream Company N.V.
target_name: The Magnum Ice Cream Company N.V.

headline: Epiroc (EPI-A) — spun off from Atlas Copco, listed Nasdaq Stockholm 18 Jun 2018
summary: Atlas Copco split into two separately listed companies. The mining and civil engineering business was carved out as Epiroc AB and distributed to shareholders 1-for-1 via a Lex Asea in-specie dividend. Epiroc began trading on Nasdaq Stockholm 18 Jun 2018 at a valuation of roughly SEK 105bn.

headline: Aumovio SE (AMV0) — spun off from Continental AG, listed Xetra Frankfurt 18 Sep 2025
summary: Continental AG completed the spin-off of its Automotive group sector as Aumovio SE. Continental shareholders received 1 Aumovio share per 2 Continental shares. Aumovio debuted on Xetra at a market cap of approximately €3.7bn on 18 Sep 2025.

headline: Titan America (TTAM) — carve-out IPO from Titan Cement (parent kept 87%), listed NYSE 7 Feb 2025
summary: Titan Cement International (Brussels/Athens: TITC) partially carved out its US cement, aggregates and concrete business as Titan America SA. IPO priced at $16 on 6 Feb 2025, raising $384M. Parent sold 15M existing shares; Titan America issued 9M new shares. Post-IPO float ~13%; Titan Cement retained ~87%. NOT a pro-rata spin-off.

headline: Canal+ SA (CAN) — spun off from Vivendi, listed LSE 16 Dec 2024
summary: Vivendi SE completed a three-way partial demerger, distributing Canal+ SA, Havas NV, and Louis Hachette Group to shareholders. Canal+ SA (pay-TV, Studiocanal) was listed on the LSE 16 Dec 2024 under ticker CAN. Vivendi shareholders received 1 Canal+ share per 1 Vivendi share. Settlement-delivery 18 Dec 2024.

headline: SKF Vertevo — planned spin-off of SKF Automotive (bearings) from AB SKF, targeting Nasdaq Stockholm Q4 2026
summary: AB SKF's board announced on 17 Sep 2024 its intention to separate the Automotive business (~SEK 25bn revenue) into a standalone listed company — later named SKF Vertevo. Targeted listing on Nasdaq Stockholm is Q4 2026, subject to AGM and regulatory approval. The remaining SKF will focus on industrial bearings, seals and lubrication.

headline: Vitesco Technologies Group AG (VTSC) — spun off from Continental AG, completed 2021-09-16
summary: Continental AG spun off its Powertrain business area as Vitesco Technologies Group AG via a statutory spin-off by absorption under German law. Continental shareholders received one Vitesco share for every five Continental shares held; Vitesco listed on the Prime Standard of the Frankfurt Stock Exchange on 16 September 2021 with a market cap of ~€2.4bn. Vitesco was subsequently acquired by Schaeffler in 2024.

headline: Daimler Truck Holding AG (DTG) — spun off from Daimler AG (now Mercedes-Benz Group AG), completed 2021-12-10
summary: Daimler AG spun off 65% of its commercial vehicle business, Daimler Truck Holding AG, distributing one Daimler Truck share for every two Daimler shares held. The resulting company is one of the world's largest commercial vehicle manufacturers and was added to the DAX in Q1 2022. Daimler AG retained 35% on listing and distributed that stake via secondary offerings over 2022-2023.

headline: TKMS AG & Co. KGaA (TKMS) — spun off from thyssenkrupp AG, completed 2025-10-20
summary: thyssenkrupp spun off 49% of its marine systems defense business (TKMS) to shareholders, with TKMS listed on the Prime Standard of the Frankfurt Stock Exchange. Shareholders received one TKMS share for every 20 thyssenkrupp shares; thyssenkrupp retains a 51% majority stake in the submarine and naval vessel manufacturer.

headline: Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG (P911) — spun off from Volkswagen AG, completed 2022-09-29
summary: Volkswagen listed 12.5% of Porsche AG's preferred shares on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange via IPO at ~€75bn valuation; Porsche Automobil Holding SE simultaneously acquired 25%+1 ordinary shares. VW paid a special dividend of ~€19.06bn from the combined proceeds to shareholders, making this a capital-return event for VW investors equivalent to a partial spin-off.
spinco_name: Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG
target_name: Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG

headline: Faurecia S.E. (distributed shares, not new spinco) (EO) — spun off from Stellantis N.V., completed 2021-03-22
summary: Stellantis N.V. (formed from PSA/FCA merger in Jan 2021) distributed its inherited 45% stake in auto-parts maker Faurecia to shareholders, plus €308m in cash proceeds from a prior stake sale. Each Stellantis shareholder received 0.017029 Faurecia shares plus €0.097 cash. Faurecia (now FORVIA) remained separately listed and was later renamed.
spinco_name: Faurecia S.E. (distributed shares, not new spinco)
target_name: Faurecia S.E. (distributed shares, not new spinco)

headline: Universal Music Group N.V. (UMG) — spun off from Vivendi SE, completed 2021-09-21
summary: Vivendi distributed 60% of Universal Music Group to its shareholders (1 UMG share per Vivendi share), listing UMG on Euronext Amsterdam at a €46bn valuation. Vivendi retained an ~18% residual stake to be sold over time, completing the full separation by end-2022. One of the largest European demerger transactions of the decade.

headline: EUROAPI SA (EAPI) — spun off from Sanofi SA, completed 2022-05-06
summary: Sanofi spun off EUROAPI, a leading European active pharmaceutical ingredients (API) contract manufacturer, via distribution of ~58% of the share capital to Sanofi shareholders at 1 share per 23 Sanofi shares. Sanofi retained ~30% and the French State (Bpifrance) acquired 12%. EUROAPI listed on Euronext Paris in May 2022 with €1bn in revenues.

headline: Pluxee N.V. (PLX) — spun off from Sodexo SA, completed 2024-02-01
summary: Sodexo spun off its employee benefits and engagement division as Pluxee N.V., listed on Euronext Paris with an implied market value of approximately €4bn. Each Sodexo shareholder received one Pluxee share, creating a standalone global employee benefits pure-play with €1bn in revenues.

headline: Havas NV (HAVAS) — spun off from Vivendi SE, completed 2024-12-16
summary: Vivendi spun off its advertising and communications agency network Havas as an independent company listed on Euronext Amsterdam, as part of Vivendi's three-way split. Each Vivendi shareholder received one Havas share, creating a standalone global advertising group with operations in 100+ countries.

headline: Louis Hachette Group SA (LHG) — spun off from Vivendi SE, completed 2024-12-16
summary: Vivendi separated Louis Hachette Group (comprising 66.53% of Lagardère SA and 100% of Prisma Media) as an independent company listed on Euronext Growth Paris. Each Vivendi shareholder received one LHG share, creating a major French publishing, distribution and travel retail group.

headline: Haleon plc (HLN) — spun off from GSK plc, completed 2022-07-18
summary: GSK demerged its Consumer Healthcare joint venture with Pfizer into Haleon plc, the world's largest pure-play consumer health company with brands including Sensodyne, Panadol and Advil. GSK shareholders received one Haleon share per GSK share held, and Haleon debuted on the LSE with a market cap of ~£30bn.

headline: Dowlais Group plc (DWL) — spun off from Melrose Industries PLC, completed 2023-04-20
summary: Melrose Industries demerged GKN Automotive, GKN Powder Metallurgy and GKN Hydrogen businesses into Dowlais Group plc, admitted to the LSE premium segment. Shareholders received one Dowlais share per Melrose share, creating a ~£2bn specialist automotive engineering company.

headline: Valterra Platinum Limited (formerly Anglo American Platinum) (VALT) — spun off from Anglo American plc, completed 2025-06-02
summary: Anglo American demerged its ~51% stake in Anglo American Platinum (renamed Valterra Platinum) to shareholders, as part of a portfolio simplification triggered by BHP's hostile takeover approach. Valterra gained a secondary listing on the LSE alongside its primary JSE listing; Anglo American conducted a concurrent share consolidation. Anglo retained ~19.9% for 90 days post-demerger.
spinco_name: Valterra Platinum Limited (formerly Anglo American Platinum)
target_name: Valterra Platinum Limited (formerly Anglo American Platinum)

headline: Primark (unnamed entity, under strategic review) (TBD) — spun off from Associated British Foods plc
summary: Associated British Foods announced a structural review of its conglomerate, with Primark (Europe's largest value fashion retailer by store count with ~£9bn revenues) under consideration for a demerger or separate listing. No formal structure or timetable confirmed as of April 2026; strategic review ongoing.

headline: Alleima AB (ALLEI) — spun off from Sandvik AB, completed 2022-08-31
summary: Sandvik distributed its Sandvik Materials Technology business area to shareholders under the new brand Alleima AB, a global manufacturer of advanced stainless steels and special alloys. Shareholders received one Alleima share for every five Sandvik shares held.

headline: Momentum Group AB (MMGR B) — spun off from Alligo AB (formerly Momentum Group AB), completed 2022-03-31
summary: Alligo AB (formerly Momentum Group) spun off its Components & Services business area as independent Momentum Group AB, listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. Shareholders received one Momentum Group share (both A and B classes) for each Alligo share held.

headline: Octave (NewCo, pending) (SDR pending) — spun off from Hexagon AB
summary: Hexagon AB announced plans to spin off its Asset Lifecycle Intelligence, Safety/Infrastructure/Geospatial divisions plus ETQ and Bricsys into a pure-play software and SaaS company called Octave, targeting listing on Nasdaq with SDRs on Nasdaq Stockholm. If shareholder-approved, effective date expected 22 May 2026 with first trading 25 May 2026 (SDRs) and 28 May 2026 (Nasdaq US shares).

headline: Kongsberg Maritime ASA (KMAR) — spun off from Kongsberg Gruppen ASA, completed 2026-04-23
summary: Kongsberg Gruppen demerged its maritime technology division (automation, navigation, dynamic positioning systems for ships) as independent Kongsberg Maritime ASA, listed on Euronext Oslo Børs. Shareholders received one KM share per Kongsberg Gruppen share; the parent retains its defense and aerospace businesses.

headline: Hermana Holding ASA (HERM) — spun off from Magnora ASA, completed 2024-06-18
summary: Magnora ASA spun off its legacy FPSO circular-hull royalty business into Hermana Holding ASA, listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange. Approximately 70% of shares were distributed to shareholders (1:1 ratio), with Magnora retaining 30% to be sold down over time; Magnora then redeployed capital into renewable energy development.

headline: Svitzer Group A/S (SVITZR) — spun off from A.P. Møller – Mærsk A/S, completed 2024-04-30
summary: A.P. Møller–Mærsk demerged its towage and marine services subsidiary Svitzer into an independent company listed on Nasdaq Copenhagen. Each APMM shareholder received one Svitzer Group share, separating the world's largest towage company with operations in 40+ countries.

headline: Mandatum plc (MANTA) — spun off from Sampo plc, completed 2023-10-02
summary: Sampo plc completed a partial demerger of its life insurance and wealth management unit as Mandatum plc, listed on Nasdaq Helsinki. Shareholders received one Mandatum share per Sampo share, allowing Sampo to become a pure P&C insurance group focused on If P&C, Topdanmark and Hastings.

headline: Easor Plc (EASOR) — spun off from Talenom Plc, completed 2026-03-02
summary: Talenom completed a partial demerger of its SaaS accounting software business (Easor Plc) onto Nasdaq Helsinki's Main Market. Each Talenom shareholder received one Easor share, separating the scalable software/fintech platform from the traditional accounting services business.

headline: Syensqo SA/NV (SYENS) — spun off from Solvay SA/NV, completed 2023-12-11
summary: Solvay split into two independent listed companies via a partial demerger under Belgian law: Syensqo received the specialty chemicals and advanced materials businesses; the legacy Solvay retained commodity chemicals. Each Solvay shareholder received one Syensqo share, with Syensqo debuting at a ~€9.5bn market cap.

headline: Sandoz Group AG (SDZ) — spun off from Novartis AG, completed 2023-10-04
summary: Novartis completed a 100% spin-off of its Sandoz generics and biosimilars division as an independent company listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange. Each Novartis shareholder received one Sandoz share for every five Novartis shares, creating a €10bn+ global generics leader.

headline: Accelleron Industries AG (ACLN) — spun off from ABB Ltd, completed 2022-10-03
summary: ABB distributed its market-leading turbocharging division (formerly ABB Turbocharging) as Accelleron Industries AG, listed on SIX Swiss Exchange. ABB shareholders received 1 Accelleron share for every 20 ABB shares held; ABB retained no stake post-spin. Accelleron focuses on high-power turbochargers for marine, energy and rail applications.

headline: Amrize Ltd (AMRZ) — spun off from Holcim Ltd, completed 2025-06-23
summary: Holcim completed a 100% spin-off of its North American building materials business as Amrize Ltd, dual-listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange and NYSE. Each Holcim shareholder received one Amrize share, creating a ~CHF26bn market-cap US-focused construction materials company.

headline: Sunrise Communications AG (SUNN) — spun off from Liberty Global Ltd, completed 2024-11-15
summary: Liberty Global spun off Sunrise, Switzerland's largest private telecoms operator, as an independent company with primary listing on the SIX Swiss Exchange. Liberty Global shareholders received Sunrise Class A shares (listed SIX) and ADSs (listed Nasdaq), separating the Swiss business from Liberty Global's broader portfolio.

headline: EuroTeleSites AG (ETS) — spun off from Telekom Austria AG, completed 2023-09-22
summary: Telekom Austria (A1 Group) spun off its cell tower and telecommunications infrastructure business (spanning Austria and CEE) as EuroTeleSites AG, listed in the Prime Market segment of the Vienna Stock Exchange. Shareholders received one EuroTeleSites share for every four Telekom Austria shares held; the spinco holds ~11,000 tower sites across the region.

headline: Iveco Group N.V. (IVG) — spun off from CNH Industrial N.V., completed 2022-01-03
summary: CNH Industrial demerged its On-Highway commercial and specialty vehicle business (Iveco trucks, FPT Industrial powertrains, Iveco Bus, Iveco Defence) into Iveco Group N.V., listed on Euronext Milan. Each CNH shareholder received one Iveco Group share, creating an independent ~€4bn revenue commercial vehicle manufacturer.

headline: Swedish forest assets NewCo (unnamed) (pending) — spun off from Stora Enso Oyj
summary: Stora Enso announced plans to separate its Swedish forest assets (>1.2 million hectares, fair value ~€5.7bn) into a new publicly listed company via a statutory partial cross-border demerger, targeting listing on Nasdaq Stockholm and Nasdaq Helsinki. Completion targeted for H1 2027; supported by major shareholders FAM AB and Solidium Oy (~55% of votes).

headline: Siemens Healthineers AG (planned spin-off, pending shareholder vote 2027) (SHL) — spun off from Siemens AG
summary: Siemens announced it will seek shareholder approval at its February 2027 AGM to spin off 30% of Siemens Healthineers shares directly to Siemens shareholders. Siemens currently holds ~75% of Healthineers (market cap ~€60bn). The spin-off would reduce Siemens' stake below controlling threshold, and Siemens has indicated it may distribute additional shares over time.
spinco_name: Siemens Healthineers AG (planned spin-off, pending shareholder vote 2027)
target_name: Siemens Healthineers AG (planned spin-off, pending shareholder vote 2027)

headline: Wickes Group plc (WIX) — spun off from Travis Perkins plc, completed 2021-04-28
summary: Travis Perkins plc demerged its DIY retail chain Wickes Group plc, which listed on the London Stock Exchange on 28 April 2021. Each Travis Perkins shareholder received one Wickes share per Travis Perkins share held. Wickes operates approximately 230 stores across the UK.

headline: Optima Health plc (OPT) — spun off from Marlowe plc, completed 2024-09-26
summary: Marlowe plc demerged its occupational health and workforce wellbeing division as Optima Health plc, which listed on AIM on 26 September 2024. Each Marlowe shareholder received one Optima Health share per Marlowe share. Optima Health is a leading UK occupational health services provider.

headline: Technip Energies N.V. (TE) — spun off from TechnipFMC plc, completed 2021-02-16
summary: TechnipFMC plc separated its engineering & construction business as Technip Energies N.V., which listed on Euronext Paris on 16 February 2021. TechnipFMC shareholders received Technip Energies shares in the split; TechnipFMC retained its subsea technologies business. TechnipFMC is incorporated in England & Wales (hence GB country code).

headline: Jackson Financial Inc. (JXN) — spun off from Prudential plc, completed 2021-09-13
summary: Prudential plc completed the separation of its US life insurance and annuities business Jackson Financial Inc., which began trading on the NYSE on 13 September 2021. Prudential shareholders received approximately 1 Jackson Financial share for every 40 Prudential shares held. Prudential retained a minority stake in Jackson post-separation.

headline: Primark (ABF Retail Holdings Ltd) (TBD) — spun off from Associated British Foods plc
summary: Associated British Foods plc announced in November 2025 a strategic review that includes the potential demerger and separate listing of Primark, its fast-fashion retail business. The transaction remains pending as of April 2026; no completion date has been confirmed. Primark operates approximately 440 stores across 17 countries.

headline: Octave (name TBC) (TBD) — spun off from Hexagon AB
summary: Hexagon AB announced in March 2025 the potential spin-off of its asset lifecycle intelligence division under the working name 'Octave'. The intended listing on Nasdaq Stockholm is expected around mid-2026, pending shareholder and regulatory approvals. Octave would include Hexagon's industrial software and digital reality solutions business.

headline: Zalando SE (share distribution) (ZAL) — spun off from Kinnevik AB, completed 2021-06-18
summary: Kinnevik AB distributed its entire 11.9% shareholding in Zalando SE to its own shareholders in-kind on 18 June 2021. Each holder of 143 Kinnevik B shares received 28 Zalando shares. This was a pure portfolio distribution rather than a corporate demerger, but it is a qualifying listed-security distribution by a public company.

headline: Polestar Automotive Holding UK PLC (SDR distribution) (PSNY) — spun off from Volvo Car AB, completed 2024-05-13
summary: Volvo Car AB distributed Swedish Depositary Receipts (SDRs) representing Polestar shares to its shareholders as part of a mandatory share redemption programme. Shareholders electing to redeem received 3 Polestar SDRs per 14 Volvo Car redemption shares. This reduced Volvo Car's stake in Polestar from approximately 48% to around 39%.
spinco_name: Polestar Automotive Holding UK PLC (SDR distribution)
target_name: Polestar Automotive Holding UK PLC (SDR distribution)

headline: Careium AB (CARE) — spun off from Doro AB, completed 2021-12-10
summary: Doro AB spun off its personal emergency response system (PERS) and digital care services business as Careium AB, listed on Nasdaq First North Sweden on 10 December 2021. Each Doro shareholder received one Careium share per Doro share. Doro retained its senior-focused mobile phone business.

headline: Cavendish Hydrogen ASA (CAVEN) — spun off from Nel ASA, completed 2024-06-12
summary: Nel ASA spun off its hydrogen fuelling station division as Cavendish Hydrogen ASA, listed on Euronext Growth Oslo on 12 June 2024. The new company focuses on hydrogen fuelling infrastructure for heavy transport. Shareholders received 1 Cavendish Hydrogen share for every 50 Nel shares held.

headline: GiG Software PLC (GIG) — spun off from Gentoo Media Inc., completed 2024-10-01
summary: Gentoo Media Inc. (formerly Gaming Innovation Group) completed a legal split of its B2B software division as GiG Software PLC on 1 October 2024. The B2C media and affiliates operations remained in Gentoo Media; the B2B platform and sportsbook technology was separated as GiG Software and continued on Nasdaq First North.

headline: Kalmar Corporation (KALMAR) — spun off from Cargotec Corporation, completed 2024-07-01
summary: Cargotec Corporation completed a partial demerger of its cargo handling equipment and automation business as Kalmar Corporation, listed on Nasdaq Helsinki on 1 July 2024. Each Cargotec shareholder received one Kalmar share per Cargotec share. Cargotec retained MacGregor (marine cargo flow) and Hiab (truck-mounted equipment).

headline: F-Secure Corporation (FSECURE) — spun off from WithSecure Corporation, completed 2022-06-30
summary: WithSecure Corporation (formerly F-Secure Corporation) completed a partial demerger of its consumer cybersecurity business as a new entity retaining the F-Secure name, listed on Nasdaq Helsinki on 30 June 2022. Each WithSecure shareholder received one F-Secure share per WithSecure share. WithSecure retained the B2B enterprise and corporate cybersecurity division.

headline: InPost S.A. (INPST) — IPO 2021-01-27 on Euronext Amsterdam
summary: Polish parcel locker and e-commerce logistics company; raised €3.2bn in the largest European IPO of 2021.

headline: Aker Horizons AS (AKH) — IPO 2021-01-28 on Euronext Oslo Børs (carve-out)
summary: Norwegian clean energy investment company carved out from Aker ASA raised ~€437m on Oslo Børs.

headline: Dr. Martens plc (DOCS) — IPO 2021-01-29 on London Stock Exchange
summary: Iconic British footwear brand raised ~£1.3bn at IPO, valuing the company at £3.7bn; PE-backed float by Permira.

headline: Moonpig Group plc (MOON) — IPO 2021-02-02 on London Stock Exchange
summary: UK online greetings card retailer raised £613m at IPO with shares surging 25% on debut.

headline: AUTO1 Group SE (AG1) — IPO 2021-02-04 on Deutsche Börse Xetra
summary: Germany's leading digital automotive platform; raised ~€1.8bn in Germany's biggest IPO since 2019.

headline: Cordiant Digital Infrastructure Ltd (CORD) — IPO 2021-02-15 on London Stock Exchange
summary: UK investment company focused on digital infrastructure assets raised ~€424m on LSE.

headline: Cint Group AB (CINT) — IPO 2021-02-19 on Nasdaq Stockholm
summary: Swedish digital insights exchange connecting researchers to respondents raised ~€594m on Stockholm.

headline: Desenio Group AB (DESENIO) — IPO 2021-02-25 on Nasdaq Stockholm
summary: Swedish online poster and art print retailer raised ~€442m on Nasdaq Stockholm.

headline: Auction Technology Group plc (ACA) — IPO 2021-02-25 on London Stock Exchange
summary: UK online marketplace for curated auctions raised ~£360m on LSE Premium segment.

headline: Aker Clean Hydrogen AS (ACH) — IPO 2021-03-05 on Euronext Oslo Børs (carve-out)
summary: Norwegian clean hydrogen spinout from Aker Horizons listed on Oslo Børs, raising ~€96m.

headline: Fix Price Group Ltd (FIXP) — IPO 2021-03-10 on London Stock Exchange
summary: Russian value retailer listed via GDRs on the London Stock Exchange under ticker FIXP, raising ~€1.48bn.

headline: Vantage Towers AG (VTWR) — IPO 2021-03-18 on Deutsche Börse Xetra (carve-out)
summary: Vodafone's European tower infrastructure company raised ~€2.2bn; largest German IPO in over two years.

headline: Trustpilot Group plc (TRST) — IPO 2021-03-23 on London Stock Exchange
summary: Danish consumer review platform raised £550m on London debut; one of UK's largest tech IPOs of 2021.

headline: FRIEDRICH VORWERK Group SE (FVW) — IPO 2021-03-23 on Deutsche Börse Xetra
summary: German energy infrastructure services company raised ~€414m on Frankfurt Prime Standard.

headline: CTP NV (CTPNV) — IPO 2021-03-25 on Euronext Amsterdam
summary: Central and Eastern European industrial real estate developer raised ~€948m; largest CEE logistics platform.

headline: Sitowise Group Oyj (SITOWS) — IPO 2021-03-29 on Nasdaq Helsinki
summary: Finnish built-environment and digitalisation consultancy; raised ~€161m total at €8.20/share at a €288m market cap on Nasdaq Helsinki.

headline: Allfunds Group plc (ALLFG) — IPO 2021-04-23 on Euronext Amsterdam
summary: Spanish B2B WealthTech platform connecting fund houses and distributors raised €2.16bn on Euronext Amsterdam.

headline: SYNLAB AG (SYNLAB) — IPO 2021-04-27 on Deutsche Börse Xetra
summary: European medical diagnostics laboratory group raised €754m on Frankfurt; PE-backed by Cinven.

headline: PolyPeptide Group AG (PPGF) — IPO 2021-04-29 on SIX Swiss Exchange
summary: Swiss-based peptide active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) CDMO raised ~€769m on SIX Exchange.

headline: Darktrace plc (DARK) — IPO 2021-05-06 on London Stock Exchange
summary: Cambridge-based AI cyber security company listed on LSE Premium segment at £1.7bn; raised £143m primary proceeds.

headline: Montana Aerospace AG (MTEC) — IPO 2021-05-12 on SIX Swiss Exchange
summary: Swiss aerospace components manufacturer raised ~€461m on SIX Exchange.

headline: SUSE S.A. (SUSE) — IPO 2021-05-19 on Deutsche Börse Xetra
summary: Enterprise open-source Linux software company (EQT-backed) raised ~€975m; first Frankfurt IPO over €1bn since 2019.

headline: Pepco Group NV (PCO) — IPO 2021-05-26 on Warsaw Stock Exchange (carve-out)
summary: Pan-European value retailer (Pepco, Poundland, Dealz) raised ~€817m in Poland; Steinhoff retained ~79%.

headline: The Italian Sea Group SpA (TISG) — IPO 2021-06-08 on Euronext Milan
summary: Italian luxury superyacht builder (Admiral brand); raised €97m total at €4.90/share on Euronext Milan MTA at a €260m market cap.

headline: Aramis Group SA (ARAMI) — IPO 2021-06-14 on Euronext Paris
summary: French multi-brand used car e-commerce platform raised ~€388m on Euronext Paris.

headline: ABOUT YOU Holding SE (YOU) — IPO 2021-06-16 on Deutsche Börse Xetra
summary: German fashion e-commerce platform raised ~€842m on Frankfurt; subsidiary of Otto Group.

headline: Elopak ASA (ELO) — IPO 2021-06-17 on Euronext Oslo Børs
summary: Norwegian renewable packaging company (cartons/liquid cartonboard); raised NOK 3.1bn in its Oslo Børs debut at NOK 28/share.

headline: Vimian Group AB (VIMIAN) — IPO 2021-06-18 on Nasdaq Stockholm
summary: Swedish veterinary pharmaceuticals and nutrition company raised ~€819m; IPO of the year for Stockholm H1.

headline: RevolutionRace AB (RRACE-B) — IPO 2021-06-18 on Nasdaq Stockholm
